title: The Lovely Lass Of Inverness.
content:
Tune - "Lass of Inverness."


I.

        The lovely lass o' Inverness,
            Nae joy nor pleasure can she see;
        For e'en and morn, she cries, alas!
            And ay the saut tear blin's her e'e:
        Drumossie moor, Drumossie day
            A waefu' day it was to me!
        For there I lost my father dear,
            My father dear, and brethren three.

II.

        Their winding sheet the bluidy clay,
            Their graves are growing green to see:
        And by them lies the dearest lad
            That ever blest a woman's e'e!
        Now wae to thee, thou cruel lord,
            A bluidy man I trow thou be;
        For mony a heart thou host made sair,
            That ne'er did wrong to thine or thee.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Lovely Lass Of Inverness."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
